_comment: Central registry of all .pkl files produced and consumed during the SOLAR pipeline. Template variables use {curly_brace} notation — these are NOT Python f-strings but documentation markers. PATH = info['PATH'] = /pnfs/ciemat.es/data/neutrinos/DUNE/SOLAR (from config/{config}/{config}_config.json). DATA_PATHS sub-keys live in config/analysis/backgrounds.json and are merged into info by load_analysis_info(). Three categories: INTERMEDIATE (read by another pipeline stage, must live in PNFS), REPRODUCIBILITY (local checkpoint arrays that persist analysis-stage selections for downstream visualisation without re-running computation — output/data/results/), OUTPUT_ONLY (write-once plots/diagnostics, never read back by pipeline).

INTERMEDIATE._comment: PKL files written by one stage and READ BY ANOTHER for computation (not only for plotting). These must be accessible to all collaborators and therefore live in PNFS.

OSCILLATION_RAW.description: High-resolution (raw) oscillation probability templates generated by prob3/nufast backends before rebinning to analysis resolution.

OSCILLATION_REBIN.description: Rebinned oscillation probability templates at analysis resolution (NADIR_BINS × energy bins). Primary input for all sensitivity computations.
OSCILLATION_REBIN.written_by: lib/oscillation.py (small_df.to_pickle → cached after first rebin of raw template)
read_by: lib/oscillation.py (pd.read_pickle → cache hit on subsequent loads)

BACKGROUND_TRUTH_PDF.description: Per-surface normalised momentum PDFs for background particles used by lib/weights.py at event-weighting time.

BACKGROUND_TRUTH_EXPOSURE.description: Per-surface exposure metadata dict (counts, livetime, geometry) used by lib/weights.py to normalise event rates.
read_by: lib/weights.py (pickle.load → exposure dict keyed by surface_id)

BACKGROUND_TRUTH_HISTOGRAMS.description: Raw per-surface (surface_id, variable) histograms before PDF normalisation. Optional: consumed by 02_background_spectra.py only when available for PDF-overlay plots.
read_by: src/physics/truth/02_background_spectra.py (optional overlay; only read if file exists)

SENSITIVITY_BACKGROUND_TEMPLATE.description: Pre-computed background event-rate array for each (NHits, AdjCl, OpHits) cut combination. Avoids re-running full background weighting during best-cut search and significance scan.

SENSITIVITY_SIGNAL_TEMPLATE.description: Pre-computed signal event-rate array for each (NHits, AdjCl, OpHits) cut × oscillation parameter grid point. Grid spans (sin²θ₁₂, Δm²₂₁) scan range.
read_by: src/physics/sensitivity/04_best_cuts.py (pd.read_pickle → chi2 optimisation at best-fit point)

SENSITIVITY_BEST_CUTS.description: Best (NHits, AdjCl, OpHits) cut combination row that maximises the sensitivity figure-of-merit. Used by all downstream significance scripts to select the optimal cut.

SENSITIVITY_RESULTS.description: Significance scan DataFrames over the (sin²θ₁₂, Δm²₂₁) parameter grid. One file per scan axis (solar/reactor × sin12/sin13) plus combined grids. Consumed by all contour and projection plots.

CALIBRATION_CHARGE.description: Charge calibration slope and intercept correction functions (scipy interp1d) for each charge cluster type. Used by lib/cluster.py for event-level charge energy calibration. MOVED FROM config/ TO PNFS so they are shared between collaborators.
read_by: lib/cluster.py (pickle.load → calibration functions applied per cluster type)

CALIBRATION_DISCRIMINANT.description: Trained random-forest classifier (sklearn RandomForestClassifier) for signal/background separation. Used by lib/cluster.py to compute per-event discriminant score. MOVED FROM config/ TO PNFS so they are shared between collaborators.

SOLAR_BACKGROUND.description: Compiled solar background DataFrame (ParticlePDG and ParticleID distributions) for a given config. Consumed by solar_background_plot.py for visualisation.

FIDUCIAL_SCAN.description: Per-(config, name, energy, folder) fiducial-volume scan DataFrame. Contains Counts/Energy/FiducializedXYZ per fiducial boundary step. Read by 02_best_fiducial.py and common/significance_plot.py to determine the optimal fiducial volume.

HEP_SIGNIFICANCE.description: Per-(config, name, energy, folder) HEP significance and counts DataFrames. Written by hep/significance_plot.py and common/significance_plot.py; consumed by hep/significance_comparison.py, hep/reference_comparison.py, common/exposure_plot.py for multi-config comparison plots.
read_by: src/physics/common/exposure_plot.py (pd.read_pickle → significance and exposure vs exposure curves)

DAYNIGHT_SIGNIFICANCE.description: Per-(config, name, energy, folder) DayNight significance and counts DataFrames. Written by common/significance_plot.py; consumed by common/exposure_plot.py for DayNight exposure curves.

SENSITIVITY_SIGNIFICANCE.description: Per-(config, name, energy, folder) Sensitivity significance and counts DataFrames plus processed contour data. Written by common/significance_plot.py and sensitivity/contour_plot.py; consumed by common/exposure_plot.py and tools/presentations/sensitivity.py.

DAYNIGHT_RESULTS.description: Full per-energy significance scan DataFrame (all cuts × exposure grid) for DayNight analysis. Primary input for all DayNight plotting scripts. Optional companion _SignificanceBins file holds per-bin breakdown when --significance_bins is active.

HEP_RESULTS.description: Full per-energy significance scan DataFrame (all cuts × exposure grid) for HEP analysis. Primary input for all HEP plotting scripts. Optional companion _SignificanceBins file holds per-bin breakdown when --significance_bins is active.
read_by: src/physics/common/exposure_plot.py (pd.read_pickle → HEP exposure and rebin modes)

BEST_SIGMAS.description: Best-significance summary DataFrame keyed by (config, name, energy): optimal NHits/OpHits/AdjCl cut, crossing exposures (Sigma2/Sigma3/PLSigma2/PLSigma3), and significance metadata. Written per analysis. The 'label' in the filename is 'highest' for the standard best-cut pass or a custom tag for spiked-curve exports.
BEST_SIGMAS.written_by: src/physics/sensitivity/05_best_sigmas.py (save_df for each analysis in --analysis list)
read_by: src/physics/common/significance_plot.py (sigma_map_path → cut selection for significance plots)
src/physics/hep/exposure_plot.py (_get_selection_cuts → cut selection for exposure curves)

SIGNAL_REBIN.description: Rebinned per-event signal (marley) or background (neutron/gamma/etc.) event arrays at analysis-energy resolution, one file per (config, name, energy, analysis). These are the primary signal/background inputs for 01_daynight.py, 01_hep.py, and their plotting scripts. Background component names (neutron, gamma, …) use the same filename pattern with the component name in place of {name}.
read_by: src/physics/hep/significance_plot.py (pd.read_pickle → counts for HEP spectrum plots)
src/physics/hep/exposure_plot.py (pd.read_pickle → rebin spectra for HEP exposure)

SIGNAL_NADIR_KDE.description: KDE-smoothed P(e|nadir) probability density files used for nadir-slice oscillation studies. One file per (name, label, nadir_slice, dm2, sin13, sin12) point. Note: nadir_slice values are stored negated (cosine convention — negative = below horizon). Currently written by 05_signal_nadir_kde.py; no active pipeline consumer identified — retained in PNFS for external analysis use.

REPRODUCIBILITY._comment: Local checkpoint arrays written to output/data/results/ after fiducialisation and cut selection. These persist the analysis-stage event selections so that oscillogram plots, significance visualisations, and other downstream tools can be reproduced without re-running the full signal processing chain. Not in PNFS — local to each workspace. Regenerate by re-running src/physics/signal/03_analysis.py.

SIGNAL_ANALYSIS_ARRAYS.description: Per-event numpy arrays (true energy, reco energy, oscillation weights, fiducial mask, cut mask) written by 03_analysis.py after fiducialisation and detector-cut selection. The '_Ref' files are the uncut reference arrays written unconditionally; the per-analysis/per-cut arrays are added when --rebin is active. All files are keyed by (config, name, folder, energy). Consumed by oscillogram_plot.py to reproduce oscillation-weighted visualisations without re-running fiducialisation.
SIGNAL_ANALYSIS_ARRAYS.written_by: src/physics/signal/03_analysis.py (save_pkl; Ref + FiducializationMask always, cut arrays only when --rebin is active)
read_by: src/physics/common/oscillogram_plot.py (_load_ref → AnalysisEnergy/Data/Weights _Ref + FiducializationMask for oscillation-weighted plot)

BEST_FIDUCIALS.description: Per-(config, analysis_key, energy) best fiducial cut JSON. Written after fiducial optimisation and consumed by 03_analysis.py, 01_background_template.py, 02_signal_template.py, and significance_plot.py to apply the optimal fiducial volume. Stored under config/analysis/fiducial/ so it is versioned alongside analysis configuration. Not a pkl — JSON for human readability.
read_by: src/physics/common/significance_plot.py (json.loads → best fiducial overlay on significance plots)
src/tools/presentations/{analysis}.py (json.loads → fiducial info for presentation slides)

OUTPUT_ONLY._comment: PKL files written by a script but never read by any other pipeline script. These are local convenience caches for plotting and can be regenerated at any time. They do NOT need to live in PNFS.

CC_FRACTION.description: Per-PDG fractional CC neutrino energy deposition as a function of neutrino energy. One row per (energy bin, PDG) combination; TSignalSumK is the fraction of neutrino energy carried by that channel. Read only by line_plot.py for a visualisation overlay — not a computation input for any pipeline stage.

SMEARING_MATRIX.description: NHits-resolved energy reconstruction smearing matrix (DataFrame of reco-energy vs true-energy bin fractions). Written once per (config, name, energy, component, NHits) combination. Used only internally by 05_smearing.py for visualisation.

EVENT_DISPLAY.description: Per-event display DataFrame for single-event visualisation tool. Written on demand, never consumed by analysis pipeline.

OSCILLOGRAM.description: Oscillogram Pee(E, θ_nadir) and day-night averaged oscillation probability DataFrames for a given oscillation parameter point, plus Signal1D_{energy} marginal spectrum DataFrames.

LINE_PLOT_DATA.description: DataFrames produced by the line-plot comparison tool. NadirPDF goes to output/data/common; KinematicThreshold goes to the same marley/stacked tree as CC_FRACTION.

BACKGROUND_SPECTRA.description: Background particle momentum spectra DataFrames produced by the background-spectra script for diagnostic visualisation. Per-config spectra go to a 'spectra/' subfolder; the global summary has no config/name prefix.
BACKGROUND_SPECTRA.written_by: src/physics/truth/02_background_spectra.py (save_pkl; per-config uses config+subfolder='spectra', global summary uses no config/name)

SIGNAL_ANALYSIS_DATA.description: Flattened per-event analysis DataFrame (energy, counts, errors, MC counts per rebin bin) saved for local inspection. Not read back by any pipeline script; use SIGNAL_REBIN (in PNFS) for computation.

SIGNAL_WEIGHTED_DISTRIBUTIONS.description: Per-NHits weighted count distribution DataFrame covering all cut combinations for a given (config, name, folder). Produced after oscillation weighting for visual inspection.

DAYNIGHT_EXPOSURE.description: Accumulated exposure vs significance records for DayNight (one row per config/name/energy/variable/spectrum_type). The legacy script writes to a local output path; common/exposure_plot.py writes to both PNFS and a local output/data/analysis/ mirror.
DAYNIGHT_EXPOSURE.in_pnfs: partial — legacy_local is not in PNFS; pnfs and local_mirror copies are written by common/exposure_plot.py

COMMON_SIGNIFICANCE_OUTPUTS.description: Per-analysis significance and counts DataFrames produced by common/significance_plot.py. *_Counts written to both PNFS and local mirror; *_Significance PNFS only. Consumed by comparison and presentation scripts only, not by any numerical computation stage. See also INTERMEDIATE entries HEP_SIGNIFICANCE, DAYNIGHT_SIGNIFICANCE, SENSITIVITY_SIGNIFICANCE.

COMMON_EXPOSURE_OUTPUTS.description: Per-analysis exposure vs significance DataFrames produced by common/exposure_plot.py for DayNight and HEP runs (Sensitivity excluded). Each file written to both PNFS and local mirror. HEP_Exposure cross-listed under INTERMEDIATE.HEP_SIGNIFICANCE.
COMMON_EXPOSURE_OUTPUTS.written_by: src/physics/common/exposure_plot.py (save_df --mode exposure; each file → {analysis}_pnfs + {analysis}_local; Sensitivity excluded)
COMMON_EXPOSURE_OUTPUTS.in_pnfs: partial — each file written to both {analysis}_pnfs and {analysis}_local

HEP_REBIN_COMPARISON.description: HEP adaptive-rebin comparison DataFrame (raw vs rebinned significance for each cut × exposure point). Saved for diagnostic review; not read back by any other script.

WORKFLOW_CORRECTION.description: NHit distribution, cluster distribution, and per-charge lifetime/correction-factor DataFrames from the charge correction stage.

WORKFLOW_CALIBRATION.description: Per-NHits 2D calibration DataFrames and per-variable 1D calibration summary DataFrames from the charge calibration stage. (The PKL calibration function files themselves are INTERMEDIATE — see CALIBRATION_CHARGE.)

WORKFLOW_DISCRIMINATION.description: Random-forest feature importance, CC production, AdjCl selection, and cluster discriminant DataFrames from the discrimination stage. (The trained model PKL itself is INTERMEDIATE — see CALIBRATION_DISCRIMINANT.)

WORKFLOW_RECONSTRUCTION.description: Neutrino energy histograms (truth, reco, 1D/2D smearing matrices, normalised fit) from the reconstruction stage. (The calibration slope/intercept PKLs at this path are INTERMEDIATE — see CALIBRATION_CHARGE.)

PDS_OPFLASH.description: MainOpFlashPE vs drift distributions and PE-cut coordinate scan DataFrames from the opflash stage.

PDS_MATCHED_EFFICIENCY.description: Matched opflash efficiency scans (drift, energy, plane, coordinate) and summary DataFrame.

PRESELECTION_PRODUCTION.description: Production distribution and limits DataFrames from the preselection production stage. One pair of files per (config, name).

PRESELECTION_EFFICIENCY.description: Per-variable preselection efficiency and combined summary DataFrames.

PRESELECTION_CLUSTERING.description: Cluster distribution and clustering efficiency DataFrames.

TPC_ELECTRON_ENERGY.description: Per-NHits electron energy resolution DataFrames and combined summary.

TPC_ADJ_CLUSTERS.description: Signal adjacent cluster counts, per-variable distributions, and summary DataFrames.

TPC_ENERGY_RESOLUTION.description: Per-NHits neutrino energy resolution DataFrames and combined summary.

VERTEX_SMEARING.description: Cumulative vertex error and vertex smearing DataFrames plus per-component smearing entries.

VERTEX_RESOLUTION.description: Vertex error (linear/log), per-coordinate vertex matrix, and combined vertex resolution DataFrames.

VERTEX_FIDUCIAL.description: Fiducial efficiency scan DataFrames (per sigma/tolerance/energy combinations).

VERTEX_RECONSTRUCTION.description: Reconstruction efficiency vs sigma/tolerance and vs energy DataFrames.
